en: Pre-employment suitability examinations must be conducted before assignment to activities with occupational disease risk. Periodic follow-up examinations must be conducted when hazardous activities continue. Examinations must address the specific health hazard.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 49(1)(1-2)

Medical examinations must address activities involving hazardous substances, physical agents, or stressful conditions. Examinations must consider associated health risks.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 51(2)

Medical examination reports must record findings and assessments. Reports must document examination outcomes. Employee medical records must include identity details, type of activity, activity dates, physician details, and examination dates.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 52(2), 58(4-6)
Examination findings must be forwarded to the labor inspectorate's medical service. Reporting must occur promptly.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 52(5)
